entries.title: TypeScript barrel-aware import graph + dotted-name resolver fix
entries.summary: Closes [#283](https://github.com/jgravelle/jcodemunch-mcp/issues/283). Investigation of NestJS's persistent `coupling = 4.4` after v1.92.0 turned up two real bugs in the import resolver, both load-bearing across every TypeScript project the observatory tracks. **Re-index

entries.title: coupling axis: filename-pattern filter for inline test conventions
entries.summary: Closes [#280](https://github.com/jgravelle/jcodemunch-mcp/issues/280). v1.91.0 fixed the coupling axis for projects that use a `tests/` directory, but left untouched ecosystems that co-locate tests with source: Go (`*_test.go`), TypeScript (`*.spec.ts`, `*.test.ts`),

entries.title: coupling axis: exclude tests/benchmarks/scripts from the metric
entries.summary: The coupling axis was structurally biased against well-tested projects. Tests, benchmark scripts, and example/script files have `Ca=0` by construction (pytest collects tests, benchmarks run from the shell, examples are illustrative) so they trivially meet the
